context: DDx:   chiasmal neuritis X; sarcoidosis; tuberculosis √  diameter of infundibulum > 4.5 mm immediately above level of dorsum; cone-shaped (on coronal scan) √  frequently calcified (teratoma) √  CSF spread (germinoma + teratocarcinoma) √  enhancement on CECT (common) √  cystic lesion containing calcifications + fat √  minimal / no contrast enhancement Age:   most common in infancy √  rim calcification + eccentric position Suprasellar Mass in Adulthood mnemonic:   SATCHMO Sarcoidosis, Sella neoplasm with superior extension Aneurysm (ectatic carotid, carotid-cavernous sinus fistula), Arachnoid cy
